markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tanglefoot Bag](/Equipment.aspx?ID=78)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 545

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A tanglefoot bag is filled with sticky substances. When you hit a creature with a tanglefoot bag, that creature takes a status penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute. Many types of tanglefoot bag also grant an item bonus on attack rolls.

 On a critical hit, a creature in contact with a solid surface becomes stuck to the surface and immobilized for 1 round, and a creature flying via wings has its wings tangled, causing it to fall safely to the ground and become unable to Fly again for 1 round. Tanglefoot bags are not effective when used on a creature that is in water.

 The target can end any effects by Escaping or spending a total of 3 [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=80) actions to carefully remove the sticky substances. These Interact actions don’t have to be consecutive, and other creatures can provide the actions as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tanglefoot Bag (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 545

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The target takes a –10-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tanglefoot Bag (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 545

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls, the target takes a –15-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 19.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tanglefoot Bag (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 545

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, the target takes a –15-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 28.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Tanglefoot Bag (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 545

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, the target takes a –20-foot penalty, and the Escape DC is 37.
name: Tanglefoot Bag

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Thunderstone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=79)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

When this stone hits a creature or a hard surface, it explodes with a deafening bang. A thunderstone deals the listed sonic damage and sonic splash damage, and each creature within 10 feet of the space in which the stone exploded must succeed at a Fortitude saving throw with the listed DC or be deafened until the end of its next turn. Many types of thunderstone grant an item bonus to attack rolls. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thunderstone (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bomb deals 1d4 sonic damage and 1 sonic splash damage, and the DC is 17.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thunderstone (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 2d4 sonic damage and 2 sonic splash damage, and the DC is 20.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Thunderstone (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 546

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 3d4 sonic damage and 3 sonic splash damage, and the DC is 28.

<title

pfs="Standard"

</title>

</traits>

The elixir of rejuvenation restores a creature to full health and eradicates toxins affecting it. When you drink this elixir, you’re restored to your maximum Hit Points, and all afflictions of 20th level or lower affecting you are removed.

 You can instead administer this elixir to a creature that has been dead for a week or less. When you do, that creature is instantly brought back to life with 1 Hit Point and no spell slots, Focus Points, or other daily resources.

**Craft Requirements** [philosopher’s stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=133), [true elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91)

[Infiltrator's Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=93)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Favored by spies and tricksters, an infiltrator’s elixir is used to alter your appearance. When imbibed, you take the shape of a humanoid creature of your size, but different enough so you might be unrecognizable. If you aren’t a humanoid, you might take on a form more similar to your own, at the GM’s discretion.

 The creator of the elixir decides the basics of the appearance you transform into when you imbibe the elixir, including eye color, skin tone, and hair color. The elixir can’t change your form into that of a specific person. After the form takes hold, you retain it for the next 10 minutes. 

 Drinking this elixir counts as setting up a disguise to Impersonate. You gain a +4 status bonus to your Deception DC to avoid others seeing through your disguise, and you add your level to this DC even if untrained. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Infiltrator's Elixir

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This tingly solution increases the elasticity and contraction of your leg muscles. For 1 minute after drinking this elixir, you can [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36) or [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) as a single action instead of 2 actions. If you do, you don’t perform the initial [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=88) (nor do you fail if you don’t Stride 10 feet).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quicksilver Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=97)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your features become thin and angular. You become swifter and nimbler, but your body also becomes fragile.

 **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) checks, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based attack rolls, and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed.

 **Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Quicksilver Mutagen

<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />

You gain inner serenity, focused on fine details and steeled against mental assaults, but you find violence off-putting.

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to Will saves and Perception, Medicine, Nature, Religion, and Survival checks. This bonus improves when you attempt Will saves against mental effects. 

**Drawback** You take a –1 penalty to attack rolls and save DCs of offensive spells, and a –1 penalty per damage die to all weapon, unarmed attack, and spell damage.

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silvertongue Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=101)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 550

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your features become striking and your voice becomes musical and commanding, though facts and figures become hazy for you and emotion clouds your ability to reason.

 **Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) checks. Your critical failures with any of these skills become failures instead.

 **Drawback** You take a –2 item penalty to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks. Choose one skill in which you are trained; for the duration, you become untrained in that skill. All your failures on checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) become critical failures.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Silvertongue Mutagen

Lethargy poison is commonly used in hit-and-run tactics by [drow](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=37) and others who want their victims alive; the ambusher retreats until the poison sets in and the victim falls unconscious. Further exposure to lethargy poison does not require the target to attempt additional saving throws; only failing an saving throw against an ongoing exposure can progress its stage.

**Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 4 hours; **Stage 1** [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) (1 round); **Stage 2** slowed 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) with no Perception check to wake up (1 round); **Stage 4** unconscious with no Perception check to wake up (1d4 hours).

</column>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />

<column gap="tiny">

Slumber wine sees its greatest use in games of intrigue, where an absence can be more devastating than injury. Characters [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=38) from slumber wine can't wake up by any means while the poison lasts, don't need to eat or drink while unconscious in this way, and appear to be recently dead unless an examiner succeeds at a DC 40 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check.

**Saving Throw** DC 32 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 hour; **Maximum Duration** 7 days; **Stage 1** unconscious (1 day); **Stage 2** unconscious (2 days); **Stage 3** unconscious (3 days).

markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 554, [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 **Activate**Interact or 1 or more days; see below --- An alchemist with the [Craft Philosopher's Stone](/Feats.aspx?ID=127) feat can create a philosopher's stone once per month by spending 1 batch of infused reagents during their daily preparations using the advanced alchemy class feature. This is the only way to create a philosopher's stone. At a glance, a philosopher's stone appears to be an ordinary, sooty piece of natural rock. Breaking the rock open with a [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=34) action (DC 35) reveals a cavity at the stone's heart. The cavity is lined with a rare type of quicksilver that can transmute base metals into precious metals or create an [elixir of rejuvenation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=92). To use the quicksilver, you must be legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) and have the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=752) feat. You can then use the stone's quicksilver for one of two effects:
  • You can apply the stone's quicksilver to an infused [true elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91) using an Interact action. This turns the elixir into an infused [elixir of rejuvenation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=92) instantaneously. This doesn't require any crafting time or additional materials.
  • You can spend up to a month of downtime applying the quicksilver either to iron to create silver or to lead to create gold. Treat this as a 20th-level task to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=23) using Crafting, except that you create 500 gp worth of your chosen metal per day on a success or 750 gp worth per day on a critical success.
name: Philosopher's Stone

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

You can slather this silvery paste onto one melee weapon, one thrown weapon, or 10 pieces of ammunition. Silversheen spoils quickly, so once you open a vial, you must use it all at once, rather than saving it. For the next hour, the weapon or ammunition counts as silver instead of its normal precious material (such as cold iron) for any physical damage it deals.

[Snake Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=136)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 554

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can apply snake oil onto a wound or other outward symptom of an affliction or condition (such as sores from a disease or discoloration from a poison). For the next hour, the symptom disappears and the wounded or afflicted creature doesn’t feel as if it still has the wound or affliction, though all effects remain. A creature can uncover the ruse by succeeding at a DC 17 Perception check, but only if it uses a Seek action to specifically examine the snake oil’s effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Snake Oil

Cold iron armor sickens certain creatures that touch it. A creature with weakness to cold iron (such as most demons and fey) that critically fails an unarmed attack against a creature in cold iron armor becomes sickened 1. A creature with weakness to cold iron is sickened 1 as long as it wears cold iron armor.

Darkwood armor is 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on armor that normally has light Bulk). It’s easier to wear than normal wood armor, reducing the Strength score necessary to ignore its check penalty by 2 and reducing its Speed penalty by 5 feet. (There are no types of wood armor in this book.)

GreenPoison

Elven chain is a chain shirt made of [mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275) that glitters in even the faintest light. Because it's constructed with small, supple rings, it has no check penalty.

 Created by elven artisans employing ancient crafting techniques, elven chain is exceptionally quiet. Unlike other chain shirts—even other mithral chain shirts—elven chain does not have the noisy trait. This suit of armor can be etched with runes like any other mithral chain shirt.

</column>

**Base Material** [Mithral](/Equipment.aspx?ID=275)

</column>

---

Mithral armor is 1 Bulk lighter than normal (or light Bulk if its normal Bulk is 1, with no effect on armor that normally has light Bulk). It’s easier to wear than normal metal armor, reducing the Strength score necessary to ignore its check penalty by 2 and reducing its Speed penalty by 5 feet.

[Magic Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=147)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk**  varies by armor
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A suit of magic armor is simply a suit of armor or explorer’s clothing etched with fundamental runes. An armor potency rune increases the armor’s item bonus to AC, and a resilient rune adds an item bonus to saving throws.

 The Prices here are for all types of armor. You don’t need to adjust the Price from leather armor to full plate or the like. These armors are made of standard materials, not precious materials such as mithral.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Magic Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Breastplate of Command](/Equipment.aspx?ID=148)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+1 resilient [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=10)_ is made from shining bronze overlaid with reinforcing golden panels in the shape of lion’s heads. Wearing this breastplate grants you a commanding aura. You gain a +2 item bonus to Diplomacy checks, but you take a –2 item penalty to Stealth checks to Hide and Sneak and Deception checks to Impersonate.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grant allies within 100 feet a +2 status bonus to saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68) effects for 1 minute. When you activate this ability, each affected ally who’s [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) reduces their frightened value by 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
Breastplate of Command
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Breastplate of Command (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 22,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The armor is a _+2 greater resilient breastplate_. The item bonus and penalty increase to +3 and –3, respectively.
name: Breastplate of Command

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Celestial Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=149)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Good" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=83" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _+2 resilient [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=9)_ is made of fine white links of a strange and slightly translucent pale metal, and the sleeves and skirt are fashioned into smaller trails that resemble feathers. Unlike normal chain mail, celestial armor has no Speed reduction, its armor check penalty is 0, and its Bulk is 1. 

 You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against fiends. You appear radiant while you wear the armor, giving you a +2 item bonus to Diplomacy checks against all creatures except fiends.

 If you are not good, you are drained 2 while wearing _celestial armor_. You can’t recover from this condition while wearing the armor.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The armor sprouts glowing wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet. The wings shed bright light in a 40-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet). The wings fade away after 10 minutes.

**Craft Requirements** You are good.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Celestial Armor

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Demon Armor](/Equipment.aspx?ID=150)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted from black iron, this crude suit of _+2 resilient [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ is designed to make you look like a horned demon, with your face peering out of the screaming maw of the beast.

While wearing the armor, you can attack with the helmet’s horns. They are a martial melee weapon with the effects of a _+2 weapon potency rune_. They deal 2d8 piercing damage and have the deadly d12 trait. On a critical hit with the horns, the target must attempt a DC 30 Fortitude save against the [_Abyssal plague_ disease](/Spells.aspx?ID=1). The horns can’t be etched with any runes. 

 If you aren’t evil, you’re drained 2 and can’t recover from this condition while wearing _demon armor_.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_dimension door_](/Spells.aspx?ID=69).

**Craft Requirements** You are evil; supply one casting of _dimension door_. 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Demon Armor

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 557 **Price** 10,000 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 4 **Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13) --- This suit of _+2 greater resilient [dragonhide](/Equipment.aspx?ID=142) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=13)_ makes you look like a fearsome dragon. The armor comes in 10 varieties corresponding to the 10 common dragon types, though other varieties undoubtedly exist. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You unleash a bout of dragon breath with a shape, damage type, and saving throw corresponding to the type of dragon used to make the armor (shown on the table below). The breath weapon deals 14d6 damage; each creature in the area must attempt a DC 36 basic saving throw. ## Dragonplate
**Dragon Type****Protects Against****Breath Weapon (Save)**
Black or copperacid30-foot line of acid (Reflex)
Blue or bronzeelectricty30-foot line of electricity (Reflex)
Brassfire30-foot line of fire (Reflex)
Greenpoison15-foot cone of poison (Fortitude)
Gold or redfire15-foot cone of fire (Reflex)
Silver or whitecold15-foot cone of Cold (Reflex)
 ## Dragonplate
**Dragon Type****Protects Against****Breath Weapon (Save)**
Black or copperacid30-foot line of acid (Reflex)
Blue or bronzeelectricty30-foot line of electricity (Reflex)
Brassfire30-foot line of fire (Reflex)
Greenpoison15-foot cone of poison (Fortitude)
Gold or redfire15-foot cone of fire (Reflex)
Silver or whitecold15-foot cone of Cold (Reflex)
 **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1,250 gp of dragonhide.
name: Dragonplate

---

This suit of _+2 resilient [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=11)_ has a large, green gemstone inset in a prominent location.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are hit or critically hit with an attack, but damage hasn’t been rolled yet;  **Effect** You force the attacker to reroll the attack roll and use the worse result. This effect has the [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) trait.

 Activating the armor causes the gemstone to turn gray and become inert. You cannot activate the armor again until the stone returns to its original green color. The gem turns green again after 1 week or when a significant foe critically succeeds at an attack roll targeting you. (If an enemy rolls a critical success at an attack against you and you force the attacker to reroll that critical success, that critical success does not recharge the mail of luck.) Even if the armor’s power returns before a week has elapsed, it can’t be activated more than once per day.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mail of Luck

---

_Ghost ammunition_ is cool to the touch. This ammunition has the benefits of the [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune and can fly through any obstacle except those that can block incorporeal creatures or effects. Though the ammunition penetrates barriers and ignores all cover, the target still benefits from the flat check from being concealed or hidden. You still can't target an undetected creature without guessing.

 After it is launched, the ammunition vanishes into mist. However, in the dead of the night 1d4 days later, it reappears in the last quiver or other container it was taken from.

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Penetrating Ammunition](/Equipment.aspx?ID=164)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 560

<row gap="medium">
**Ammunition** [arrow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=83), [bolt](/Weapons.aspx?ID=81)

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ammunition has a slender shape and a viciously pointed tip. When you activate and shoot _penetrating ammunition_, the Strike takes the shape of a 60-foot line originating from you. Roll one attack roll and compare the result to the AC of each target in the line. The ammunition ignores up to 10 of a target’s resistance, and it can penetrate walls up to 1 foot thick with Hardness 10 or less. Each target that takes damage from this ammunition also takes 1d6 persistent bleed damage.

 If your attack roll result is a natural 20, you improve your degree of success only against the first target in the line, but you can still score a critical hit on other targets if your result exceeds their AC by 10 or more. If you have access to your bow’s critical specialization effect, penetrating ammunition applies that effect only against a target in the last square of the line.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Penetrating Ammunition

A creature can remove the ammunition with an Interact action, but the ammunition itself continues to glow for the rest of the duration or until destroyed.

<traits>

---

Mystic patterns create a magic reservoir within this ammunition. You activate _spellstrike ammunition_ by Casting a Spell into the ammunition. The spell must be of a spell level the ammunition can hold, and the spell must be able to target a creature other than the caster. A creature hit by activated _spellstrike ammunition_ is targeted by the spell. If the creature isn’t a valid target for the spell, the spell is lost.

 The ammunition affects only the target hit, even if the spell would normally affect more than one target. If the spell requires a spell attack roll, use the result of your ranged attack roll with the ammunition to determine the degree of success of the spell. If the spell requires a saving throw, the target attempts the save against your spell DC.

 The maximum level of spell the ammunition can hold determines its item level and Price.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spellstrike Ammunition

When an activated _storm arrow_ hits a target, it is buffeted by raging winds and struck by a bolt of lightning that deals 3d12 electricity damage and the target must attempt a DC 25 Reflex saving throw. If this arrow is shot from a weapon with a [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=302) property rune, the save DC increases to 27, though the attack doesn’t benefit from the _shock_ property rune itself. **Critical Success** The foe is unaffected.
**Success** The foe takes half damage and isn’t affected by the wind.
**Failure** The foe takes full damage and is buffeted by winds for 1 round, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls and a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its fly Speed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the foe takes double damage.

After an activated _viper arrow_ hits a target, the arrow transforms into a [viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=379). The target is affected by the viper's poison, as if it had been bitten. The viper then lands in an open space adjacent to the target.

 The viper has the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=154) trait and acts at the end of your turn, even though you didn't use the Sustain a Spell action. It is under the GM's control, but it generally attacks the creature the arrow struck. The viper vanishes after 1 minute or when slain.

    level="1"
    right="Item 15"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Obfuscation Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=175)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 561

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You can spread this blue-gray gel on a single item with a Bulk of 3 or less to ward it against magical detection. It becomes immune to divination magic of 8th level or lower (such as [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=273)). This oil is permanent, but it can be removed with acid. Removing the oil in this way usually takes 1 minute for objects with Bulk of 1 or less, or a number of minutes equal to the item’s Bulk.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Obfuscation Oil

[Oil of Potency](/Equipment.aspx?ID=179)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

When you apply this thick, viscous oil to a weapon or suit of armor, that item immediately becomes magically potent. If the item is a weapon, it temporarily becomes a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=280) weapon_, or, if it's armor, it temporarily becomes _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=279) armor_. This lasts for 1 minute.

</column>

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 175 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- This oil contains magnetically charged iron filings repelled into opposite ends of the vial. For 1 minute after you apply this oil to armor, any creature that hits you with a melee Strike must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save with the following effects. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is pushed up to 10 feet away from you (the GM determines the direction).
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the creature is also knocked prone.
name: Oil of Repulsion

markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- This liquid contains blood from a certain type of dragon. For 1 hour after you imbibe the concoction, you can unleash a breath weapon used by that type of dragon. The potency of the breath depends on the potion’s type, based on the age of the dragon whose blood was used to make the potion. This potion has the trait matching the damage type of the breath weapon. Exhaling dragon breath uses a single action. The damage type and the area of the dragon breath depend on the type of dragon blood in the potion, as shown in the table below. Regardless of the dragon type, the breath weapon deals 4d6 damage, and each creature in the area must attempt a DC 23 basic save of a type determined by the type of the dragon. After you use the breath weapon, you can’t do so again for 1d4 rounds. ## Dragon's Breath Potion
**Dragon Type****Breath Weapon (Save)**
Black or copper30-foot line of acid (Reflex)
Blue or bronze30-foot line of electricity (Reflex)
Brass30-foot line of fire (Reflex)
Green15-foot cone of poison (Fortitude)
Gold or red15-foot cone of fire (Reflex)
Silver or white15-foot cone of cold (Reflex)
 Dragon's Breath Potion (Young) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 70 gp Dragon's Breath Potion (Adult) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 400 gp --- The damage is 6d6 and the save DC is 29. Dragon's Breath Potion (Wyrm) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 3,000 gp --- The damage is 10d6 and the save DC is 37.
name: Dragon's Breath Potion

markdown:
**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**Interact --- This liquid contains blood from a certain type of dragon. For 1 hour after you imbibe the concoction, you can unleash a breath weapon used by that type of dragon. The potency of the breath depends on the potion’s type, based on the age of the dragon whose blood was used to make the potion. This potion has the trait matching the damage type of the breath weapon. Exhaling dragon breath uses a single action. The damage type and the area of the dragon breath depend on the type of dragon blood in the potion, as shown in the table below. Regardless of the dragon type, the breath weapon deals 4d6 damage, and each creature in the area must attempt a DC 23 basic save of a type determined by the type of the dragon. After you use the breath weapon, you can’t do so again for 1d4 rounds. ## Dragon's Breath Potion
**Dragon Type****Breath Weapon (Save)**
Black or copper30-foot line of acid (Reflex)
Blue or bronze30-foot line of electricity (Reflex)
Brass30-foot line of fire (Reflex)
Green15-foot cone of poison (Fortitude)
Gold or red15-foot cone of fire (Reflex)
Silver or white15-foot cone of cold (Reflex)
 Dragon's Breath Potion (Young) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 70 gp Dragon's Breath Potion (Adult) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 400 gp --- The damage is 6d6 and the save DC is 29. Dragon's Breath Potion (Wyrm) **Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 562 **Price** 3,000 gp --- The damage is 10d6 and the save DC is 37.
name: Dragon's Breath Potion (Adult)

You also gain the effects of a 4th-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell, which protects against [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271) spells of 8th level and lower and has a DC of 36 against [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344). The potion’s effects last for 10 minutes.

</column>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Upon drinking this potion, your biology instantly transforms to take on a set of sexual characteristics of your choice, changing your appearance and physiology accordingly. You have mild control over the details of this change, but you retain a strong “family resemblance” to your former appearance.

 The magic functions instantaneously and can’t be counteracted. Your new anatomy is as healthy and functional as your previous body’s, potentially allowing you to procreate (depending on your ancestry’s biology). Drinking a subsequent _serum of sex shift_ allows you to either revert back to your original form or adopt other sexual characteristics, as you choose. The elixir has no effect if you are pregnant or from an ancestry with no sexual differentiation. Most ancestries have a wide spectrum of sexual differentiation, some common, others more rare.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Serum of Sex Shift

Upon drinking the potion, attempt a DC 19 Will save. You can voluntarily fail or critically fail. **Success** The potion does not affect you.
**Failure** When you speak, you must tell the truth.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and when someone asks you a question, you must attempt another DC 19 Will saving throw. If you fail this saving throw, you must answer the question truthfully if you are able to do so; if you succeed, you are temporarily immune to further attempts to ask the same question within the potion’s duration.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Effervescent Ampoule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=207)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 566

**Price** 7 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

**Requirement** You're an expert in Acrobatics
</row>

</column>

---

Light spring water fizzes and bubbles within this small glass globe, spilling onto the affixed armor when activated. Until the end of your turn, the armor lets you move across water and other liquids as if they were solid ground. If you Stride or Step over a weight-sensitive pressure plate, you don’t cause the plate to depress, which prevents you from triggering any device or hazard attached to the pressure plate. When the ampoule’s effect ends, you sink, fall, break through flimsy ground, or land on pressure plates as normal for your current location.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Effervescent Ampoule
requirement_markdown: You're an expert in Acrobatics

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emerald Grasshopper](/Equipment.aspx?ID=208)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 566

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You attempt a High Jump but haven't rolled yet

**Requirement** You're an expert in Athletics
</row>

</column>

---

This metal grasshopper studded with emeralds is usually clasped to the legs of a suit of armor. When you activate it, if you succeed at the Athletics check, you Leap up to 50 feet vertically and up to 10 feet horizontally. If you critically succeed, you can Leap up to 75 feet vertically and 20 feet horizontally. If you don’t end your jump on solid ground, you flutter in the air until the end of your turn, then fall harmlessly at a rate of 60 feet per round until you reach the ground.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Emerald Grasshopper
requirement_markdown: You're an expert in Athletics

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

</row>

---

This piece of bronzite is shaped like an oval with points at both ends. It has a carved flame at its center and is traditionally worn over the heart. You can activate only one _flame navette_ per day. When you activate the navette, you gain the benefit of the fighter’s [Determination](/Feats.aspx?ID=415) class feat, with a counteract modifier of +22. If you have the Determination feat, you can use your own modifier if it’s better.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wolf Fang](/Equipment.aspx?ID=239)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=155" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>envision</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You successfully Trip a foe

**Requirement** You are trained in Athletics
</row>

</column>

---

This wolf canine is bound in a strip of leather and tied to a buckle or strap of a suit of armor. When you activate the fang, you deal bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier to the target of your Trip. If your Trip would already deal physical damage that doesn’t include an ability modifier, add your Strength modifier to that damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Wolf Fang
requirement_markdown: You are trained in Athletics

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Candle of Truth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=240)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 75 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tapered candle has a golden wick that burns with white fire. You activate the candle by lighting it, which causes creatures within 10 feet of the candle to find it difficult to tell falsehoods. Creatures in the area receive a –4 status penalty to Lie.

 In addition, when first entering the affected area, each creature (including you) must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or be unable to tell any deliberate lies while within 10 feet of the lit candle. This lasts for as long as the candle is lit. Once lit, the candle burns for 10 minutes, and it cannot be extinguished.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Candle of Truth

    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dust of Appearance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=241)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stored in a small reed, this powder looks like a fine metallic dust. When you fling it in the air, it coats all creatures in a 10-foot burst centered on a point within 5 feet of you. For 1 minute, the coated creatures can’t be concealed or invisible, nor can they benefit from [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197) or similar abilities that create illusory duplicates. Any illusions in the area of 3rd level or lower are revealed as such, although this does not end their effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dust of Appearance

>
[Dust of Disappearance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=242)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

---

This powder shimmers like a thousand tiny motes of light. Activating the dust by sprinkling it on yourself or a creature within reach casts a 4th-level [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164) spell with a duration of 1 minute on that creature. This invisibility can’t be negated or seen through by any spell of 3rd level or lower or any item of 5th level or lower.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Elemental Gem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=243)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 200 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>command, Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You shout the name of an elemental lord and dash this glassy gem against a hard surface to activate it. It cracks open, casting a 5th-level [_summon elemental_](/Spells.aspx?ID=320) spell to summon forth an elemental you control as long as you spend an action each round to Sustain the Activation. 

 This gem comes in four varieties: transparent for a living whirlwind, light brown for a living landslide, reddish orange for a living wildfire, and blue-green for a living waterfall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Elemental Gem

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Feather Token](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each feather token appears to be a simple feather from some exotic bird. The feather’s shaft, dipped in gold, bears a single arcane rune. Activating a feather token causes it to transform into another object, which then can be used as normal for that object. Each feather can be activated only once, with most of them permanently becoming the item in their description.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Anchor)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only on a boat. When activated, this feather transforms into a massive anchor that causes the boat to immediately stop. After 1 day, the anchor vanishes and the boat can move as normal. The anchor is attached to the boat by a magical chain of force, but the chain can be removed by [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) or destroyed (Hardness 30, HP 40).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Bird)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this token transforms into a small sparrow that waits on your finger for you to relay a message up to 1 minute in length, along with the name and rough location of a recipient. The recipient must be someone you have met, and the location must be somewhere you have visited. After receiving the message, the bird flies off to deliver it, traveling at 30 miles per hour and then searching for the target in the location you provided. If the bird finds the target, it moves adjacent to them, your words emanate from the bird, and the bird then flies off and vanishes. The bird also vanishes if it fails to find your target after 10 hours of searching the location you specified.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Chest)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you use this token, a small wooden chest appears and immediately opens. This chest can hold up to 10 Bulk worth of items. Once the chest is closed, it transforms again—this time into a key—taking all of the stored items with it. You can activate this key by spending an Interact action to turn it in an imaginary lock, which causes it to transform back into a chest with all of the items still inside. Once it has turned into a chest a second time, it forever remains a wooden chest.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Fan)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Activating this feather requires you to fan it in a given direction. If this direction is toward the sail of a vessel, the feather flutters up toward the sail and fans continuously, filling the sail with air and granting the vessel a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. If fanned in any other direction, it instead produces a single casting of [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (DC 20).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Holly Bush)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When this feather is activated, it immediately transforms into a living holly bush, filling a single square. This bush can provide standard cover. In addition, the bush has 2d4 bright-red berries. While holly berries are usually poisonous, these berries are infused with beneficial magic. You can pick and eat a berry as an Interact action to recover 1 Hit Point. Once plucked from the bush, a berry becomes non-magical after a few seconds, so it doesn’t heal you if you don’t eat it within the span of your Interact action.

 If activated on soil, the plant continues to grow and thrive (although it doesn’t produce any more healing berries). If activated elsewhere, it withers and dies within 1d4 days.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Ladder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this feather transforms permanently into a 20-foot-long wooden ladder.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Swan Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only when tossed into a large body of water, such as a lake or broad river. It transforms into a swan-shaped boat capable of carrying up to 32 Medium creatures, 8 Large creatures, or 2 Huge creatures. The boat moves on the water at a Speed of 50 feet, and lasts 1 day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Tree)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 38 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This token can be activated only on an unoccupied patch of earth or soil. When activated, this token transforms into an oak tree, 60 feet tall with a 5-foot-wide trunk. The tree continues to live and grow if conditions are favorable.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Whip)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather transforms into a _+1 striking [dancing whip_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=293) when activated. The whip immediately jumps from your grasp and proceeds to attack your enemies until 1 minute has passed or it fails its flat check for _dancing_, at which point it vanishes. If the whip’s target isn’t prone, the whip uses its actions to attempt to Trip that creature instead of making a Strike. The whip’s total attack modifier for Striking or Tripping is +18 instead of the normal bonus for a _dancing_ weapon.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Balloon)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can't be activated underwater. When activated, the feather transforms into a balloon of waxed cloth that is 3 feet in diameter and filled with hot air. Handles on the bottom of the balloon allow up to two creatures sized Medium or smaller to hold onto the balloon. One round after being activated, the balloon rises up into the air at a speed of 20 feet per round. After rising for 3 rounds, the balloon floats in place for two rounds as the air inside cools, then descends at a speed of 20 feet per round. When the balloon reaches its original elevation, the balloon deflates.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Puddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, the feather transforms into two gallons of fresh, clear water. If no one drinks it, the water evaporates after 1 day.
name: Feather Token

right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Swan Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only when tossed into a large body of water, such as a lake or broad river. It transforms into a swan-shaped boat capable of carrying up to 32 Medium creatures, 8 Large creatures, or 2 Huge creatures. The boat moves on the water at a Speed of 50 feet, and lasts 1 day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Tree)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 38 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This token can be activated only on an unoccupied patch of earth or soil. When activated, this token transforms into an oak tree, 60 feet tall with a 5-foot-wide trunk. The tree continues to live and grow if conditions are favorable.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Whip)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather transforms into a _+1 striking [dancing whip_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=293) when activated. The whip immediately jumps from your grasp and proceeds to attack your enemies until 1 minute has passed or it fails its flat check for _dancing_, at which point it vanishes. If the whip’s target isn’t prone, the whip uses its actions to attempt to Trip that creature instead of making a Strike. The whip’s total attack modifier for Striking or Tripping is +18 instead of the normal bonus for a _dancing_ weapon.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Balloon)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can't be activated underwater. When activated, the feather transforms into a balloon of waxed cloth that is 3 feet in diameter and filled with hot air. Handles on the bottom of the balloon allow up to two creatures sized Medium or smaller to hold onto the balloon. One round after being activated, the balloon rises up into the air at a speed of 20 feet per round. After rising for 3 rounds, the balloon floats in place for two rounds as the air inside cools, then descends at a speed of 20 feet per round. When the balloon reaches its original elevation, the balloon deflates.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Puddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, the feather transforms into two gallons of fresh, clear water. If no one drinks it, the water evaporates after 1 day.
name: Feather Token (Anchor)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Feather Token](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each feather token appears to be a simple feather from some exotic bird. The feather’s shaft, dipped in gold, bears a single arcane rune. Activating a feather token causes it to transform into another object, which then can be used as normal for that object. Each feather can be activated only once, with most of them permanently becoming the item in their description.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Anchor)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only on a boat. When activated, this feather transforms into a massive anchor that causes the boat to immediately stop. After 1 day, the anchor vanishes and the boat can move as normal. The anchor is attached to the boat by a magical chain of force, but the chain can be removed by [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) or destroyed (Hardness 30, HP 40).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Bird)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this token transforms into a small sparrow that waits on your finger for you to relay a message up to 1 minute in length, along with the name and rough location of a recipient. The recipient must be someone you have met, and the location must be somewhere you have visited. After receiving the message, the bird flies off to deliver it, traveling at 30 miles per hour and then searching for the target in the location you provided. If the bird finds the target, it moves adjacent to them, your words emanate from the bird, and the bird then flies off and vanishes. The bird also vanishes if it fails to find your target after 10 hours of searching the location you specified.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Chest)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you use this token, a small wooden chest appears and immediately opens. This chest can hold up to 10 Bulk worth of items. Once the chest is closed, it transforms again—this time into a key—taking all of the stored items with it. You can activate this key by spending an Interact action to turn it in an imaginary lock, which causes it to transform back into a chest with all of the items still inside. Once it has turned into a chest a second time, it forever remains a wooden chest.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Fan)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Activating this feather requires you to fan it in a given direction. If this direction is toward the sail of a vessel, the feather flutters up toward the sail and fans continuously, filling the sail with air and granting the vessel a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. If fanned in any other direction, it instead produces a single casting of [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (DC 20).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Holly Bush)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When this feather is activated, it immediately transforms into a living holly bush, filling a single square. This bush can provide standard cover. In addition, the bush has 2d4 bright-red berries. While holly berries are usually poisonous, these berries are infused with beneficial magic. You can pick and eat a berry as an Interact action to recover 1 Hit Point. Once plucked from the bush, a berry becomes non-magical after a few seconds, so it doesn’t heal you if you don’t eat it within the span of your Interact action.

 If activated on soil, the plant continues to grow and thrive (although it doesn’t produce any more healing berries). If activated elsewhere, it withers and dies within 1d4 days.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Ladder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this feather transforms permanently into a 20-foot-long wooden ladder.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Swan Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

It transforms into a swan-shaped boat capable of carrying up to 32 Medium creatures, 8 Large creatures, or 2 Huge creatures. The boat moves on the water at a Speed of 50 feet, and lasts 1 day.

If this direction is toward the sail of a vessel, the feather flutters up toward the sail and fans continuously, filling the sail with air and granting the vessel a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. If fanned in any other direction, it instead produces a single casting of [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (DC 20).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Holly Bush)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When this feather is activated, it immediately transforms into a living holly bush, filling a single square. This bush can provide standard cover. In addition, the bush has 2d4 bright-red berries. While holly berries are usually poisonous, these berries are infused with beneficial magic. You can pick and eat a berry as an Interact action to recover 1 Hit Point. Once plucked from the bush, a berry becomes non-magical after a few seconds, so it doesn’t heal you if you don’t eat it within the span of your Interact action.

 If activated on soil, the plant continues to grow and thrive (although it doesn’t produce any more healing berries). If activated elsewhere, it withers and dies within 1d4 days.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Ladder)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this feather transforms permanently into a 20-foot-long wooden ladder.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Swan Boat)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 76 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only when tossed into a large body of water, such as a lake or broad river. It transforms into a swan-shaped boat capable of carrying up to 32 Medium creatures, 8 Large creatures, or 2 Huge creatures. The boat moves on the water at a Speed of 50 feet, and lasts 1 day.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Tree)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 38 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This token can be activated only on an unoccupied patch of earth or soil. When activated, this token transforms into an oak tree, 60 feet tall with a 5-foot-wide trunk. The tree continues to live and grow if conditions are favorable.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Whip)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 130 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather transforms into a _+1 striking [dancing whip_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=293) when activated. The whip immediately jumps from your grasp and proceeds to attack your enemies until 1 minute has passed or it fails its flat check for _dancing_, at which point it vanishes. If the whip’s target isn’t prone, the whip uses its actions to attempt to Trip that creature instead of making a Strike. The whip’s total attack modifier for Striking or Tripping is +18 instead of the normal bonus for a _dancing_ weapon.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 6"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Balloon)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can't be activated underwater. When activated, the feather transforms into a balloon of waxed cloth that is 3 feet in diameter and filled with hot air. Handles on the bottom of the balloon allow up to two creatures sized Medium or smaller to hold onto the balloon. One round after being activated, the balloon rises up into the air at a speed of 20 feet per round. After rising for 3 rounds, the balloon floats in place for two rounds as the air inside cools, then descends at a speed of 20 feet per round. When the balloon reaches its original elevation, the balloon deflates.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Feather Token (Puddle)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Special: Fumbus](/Sources.aspx?ID=105)

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, the feather transforms into two gallons of fresh, clear water. If no one drinks it, the water evaporates after 1 day.
name: Feather Token (Fan)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Feather Token](/Equipment.aspx?ID=244)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each feather token appears to be a simple feather from some exotic bird. The feather’s shaft, dipped in gold, bears a single arcane rune. Activating a feather token causes it to transform into another object, which then can be used as normal for that object. Each feather can be activated only once, with most of them permanently becoming the item in their description.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Anchor)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This feather can be activated only on a boat. When activated, this feather transforms into a massive anchor that causes the boat to immediately stop. After 1 day, the anchor vanishes and the boat can move as normal. The anchor is attached to the boat by a magical chain of force, but the chain can be removed by [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78) or destroyed (Hardness 30, HP 40).

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Bird)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 8 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When activated, this token transforms into a small sparrow that waits on your finger for you to relay a message up to 1 minute in length, along with the name and rough location of a recipient. The recipient must be someone you have met, and the location must be somewhere you have visited. After receiving the message, the bird flies off to deliver it, traveling at 30 miles per hour and then searching for the target in the location you provided. If the bird finds the target, it moves adjacent to them, your words emanate from the bird, and the bird then flies off and vanishes. The bird also vanishes if it fails to find your target after 10 hours of searching the location you specified.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Chest)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you use this token, a small wooden chest appears and immediately opens. This chest can hold up to 10 Bulk worth of items. Once the chest is closed, it transforms again—this time into a key—taking all of the stored items with it. You can activate this key by spending an Interact action to turn it in an imaginary lock, which causes it to transform back into a chest with all of the items still inside. Once it has turned into a chest a second time, it forever remains a wooden chest.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Feather Token (Fan)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 570

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

If this direction is toward the sail of a vessel, the feather flutters up toward the sail and fans continuously, filling the sail with air and granting the vessel a +10-foot circumstance bonus to its Speed for 8 hours. If fanned in any other direction, it instead produces a single casting of [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143) (DC 20).
